title: Fed’s Miran says he may trim rate cut outlook, citing inflation
content:
Here's a summary of the headline in three bullet points:

*   A Federal Reserve official, Miran, has indicated he might lower his expectations for future interest rate cuts.
*   The primary reason cited for this potential shift is persistent concerns about inflation.
*   This suggests a more cautious approach to monetary policy, potentially leading to fewer or delayed rate reductions than previously anticipated.

title: India better placed to weather West Asia war impact: Former World Bank official
content:
India is considered well-prepared to mitigate the effects of a potential war in West Asia.
*   The country's resilience suggests it can effectively manage the economic and geopolitical impacts.
*   This optimistic assessment comes from a former World Bank official.

title: Supreme Court refuses to interfere with Bombay HC verdict lifting stay on fraud proceedings against Anil Ambani
content:
The Supreme Court has refused to interfere with a Bombay High Court verdict.
*   The Bombay High Court's verdict had lifted a stay on fraud proceedings.
*   As a result, fraud proceedings against Anil Ambani can now continue.

title: Sebi extends registration validity period for not-for-profit entities on Social Stock Exchange
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   Sebi has extended the validity period for entity registration.
*   This extension specifically applies to not-for-profit organizations.
*   The affected entities are those operating on the Social Stock Exchange.

title: Russia hits Ukraine with missiles & drones, 17 dead
content:
Russia launched strikes against Ukraine.
*   The strikes involved both missiles and drones.
*   The attacks resulted in 17 fatalities.

title: Pension Schemes Bill: MPs back powers to steer private pensions; House of Lords to review next week
content:
MPs have approved new powers under the Pension Schemes Bill aimed at influencing private pension schemes.
*   These powers would give Parliament the ability to "steer" the direction of private pensions.
*   The Bill will proceed to the House of Lords for its review, scheduled to take place next week.

title: Tamil Nadu election: Biryani caterers work around fuel shortages, inflation for Tiruchi’s political meetings
content:
Biryani caterers in Tiruchi are busy supplying food for political meetings during the Tamil Nadu election.
*   They are facing significant operational challenges due to widespread fuel shortages and general inflation.
*   Despite these hurdles, the caterers are finding ways to work around the difficulties to continue their services.
